Los Mazatlecos.description: Los Mazatlecos is an enforcer gang of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel, based in Mazatlán, Sinaloa, Mexico. Formed around 2000 by Alfredo Beltrán Leyva, the group is one of the largest gangs working for the cartel. They are engaged in a turf war against the Sinaloa Cartel and are responsible for numerous deaths and kidnappings in Sinaloa and Durango. The gang is currently led by Fausto Isidro Meza Flores.

Kozakura-ikka.description: Yakuza organization based in Kagoshima, Kyushu, Japan, with about 40 active members. Formed in 1948 as the Kozakura-gumi by Seizo Osato. Known for its 'Kozakura Monroe Doctrine' policy of not operating outside Kagoshima Prefecture and barring other syndicates from entering. Engages in protection racketeering, drug trafficking, and unlawful involvement in public works.

descriptions: Hiraoka-gumi is a sub-group of Kozakura-ikka, as its leader became the fourth president of the parent organization.
edges.evidence_quote: 4th (1988-): Kiei Hiraoka (平岡 喜榮) who was 1st kumicho of the Hiraoka-gumi.

Woolwich Boys.description: Organised street gang based in Woolwich, south-east London, founded in the late 1990s. Initially a youth gang involved in street crime, it evolved into a structured criminal organization engaged in drug trafficking, contract killing, and murder. The gang has an estimated 300 members, predominantly Somali, and remains active.

Winter Hill Gang.description: The Winter Hill Gang was a loose confederation of American organized crime figures based in the Boston area, primarily active from 1961 to 2000. Founded by James 'Buddy' McLean and Howard 'Howie' Winter, it was predominantly Irish-American but included members of other ethnicities. The gang engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, gambling, and weapons smuggling, and was the second most powerful criminal organization in New England after the Patriarca crime family. It was taken over by James 'Whitey' Bulger in 1979 and eventually dissolved by 2000.
